rules.rule_text:
title: Phishing Lure via Google Translate
id: 910e7e3d-794c-4ec2-b94f-9cbb1857b50c
description: It is common for phishing lures to be served from IPFS or object storage
  services, using Google Translate as a proxy
status: stable
author: NTT Security - Patrik Olson
date: '2022-11-21'
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  google_translate:
  - cs-host: translate.goog
  - cs-host|endswith: .translate.goog
  sus_ipfs:
    c-uri-query|contains: _x_tr_hp=bafy
  sus_storage_bucket:
    cs-host|contains:
    - linodeobjects-com
    - digitaloceanspaces-com
    - dream-io
    - wasabisys-com
    - vultrobjects-com
    - backblazeb2-com
    - storageapi-fleek-co
    - blob-core-windows-net
    - r2-dev
  sus_aws_s3:
    cs-host|contains: amazonaws-com
    cs-host|re: \bs3\b
  condition: google_translate and 1 of sus*
falsepositives:
- Unlikely, but do verify that the url is serving a phishing page
level: high
tags:
- attack.initial_access
